title: salix × margarita
intro: Salix × margarita, also known as the Creeping Willow or Margarita Willow, is a hybrid plant resulting from the cross between Salix matsudana 'Tortuosa' and Salix alba. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties due to the presence of salicylic acid and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Salix × margarita include reducing pain and inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting digestive health, making it a popular natural remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of Salix × margarita can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, often used to treat conditions such as arthritis, fever, and skin issues.
